We recommended and
Management agreed to strictly
adhere to the guidelines under
COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the
granting of cash advance and its
liquidation and COA Circular No.
2015-009 through:
a) Instructing the Municipal
Accountant, the Municipal
Budget Officer, and the
Municipal Treasurer to refrain
from processing disbursement for
vouchers of, approve updated
audit obligations to, and prepare
checks for, a new cash advance
of an employee until his/her
previous cash advance has been
settled, respectively and refrain
from granting cash advances
that are above the approved
maximum cash accountability
of accountable officer’s fidelity
bond;
b) Designating additional
disbursing officers to prevent
granting of multiple cash
advances to one disbursing
officer only;
c) Instructing the Municipal
Accountant to (i) refrain from
processing single cash advance
with various purposes; and (ii)
record the grant and liquidation
of cash advances in the proper
accounts as prescribed under
Annex B of COA Circular No.
2015-009 prescribing the Chart
of Accounts for Local
Government Units; and
d) Directing the disbursing
officers to strictly liquidate
cash advances granted to them
within the prescribed timeline.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to regularly review the
sufficiency of fidelity bonds of the SDOs prior to the granting of cash advance to
ensure adequate coverage of their cash accountability. Likewise, we also
recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to perform verification of the
sufficiency of fidelity bonds prior to the approval of disbursement for cash advances.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Local Chief Executive designate an inspection committee in the assessment
of supplies upon receipt;
b. Distribution Units/Requisitioning Units regularly prepare and submit RIS
and SSMI pertaining to issuances of supplies to the accounting section; and
c. Accountant coordinate with the distribution units on a regular basis to
enforce the preparation and submission of RIS and SSMI on the issuance
of supplies for timely recording in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. Accounting Unit, GSO, and RHU regularly coordinate and reconcile the
receipts and issuances of donated supplies to ensure timely recognition in
the books and inventory monitoring; and
b. Accounting Unit to draw an entry to record the donations received and
their issuance.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
 Inventory committee to submit RPCPPE in the prescribed form and include
in the count the immovable PPEs to ascertain their status and conditions and
forward the report to the accounting section; and
 Accountant, upon receipt of the report of the inventory committee, to draw
necessary journal entries to reflect the correct valuation of the assets.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
 exert effort in locating the details of the unaccounted beginning balances,
update the books and records, and effect necessary adjustments;
 in coordination with General Services Office (GSO) reconcile the accounting
books and property records and investigate and institute appropriate action
for any deficiencies noted; and
 forward the result of the above to the Inventory committee to locate the PPE
and validate its condition and existence.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
 designate a responsible officer or task force to expedite the approval and
acquisition of license to sell; and
 direct the MPDO to conduct a post-implementation review to identify lapses
in project execution and ensure future projects undergo comprehensive
feasibility studies and regulatory assessments before fund utilization to
prevent similar licensing delays.

We recommended and the GSO agreed to conduct further investigation for the
shortage reported in the RPCPPE and impose commensurate liability of end-users on
said loss, if warranted.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
 GSO cause the immediate preparation of IIRUP and IIRUSP and submission
thereof to the Disposal Committee;
 Disposal Committee upon receipt of IIRUP to facilitate the speedy disposal of
unserviceable properties; and
 Accountant to update financial records upon disposal.

We recommended and the GSO agreed to:
a) cause the registration of all motor vehicles owned by the municipality with
LTO; and
b) facilitate the proper documentation for transferred/donated units and
evaluation of vehicle roadworthiness to facilitate management decision on re-
registration, decommissioning or disposal.

We recommended and the GSO agreed to facilitate the insurance of all motor
vehicles owned by the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that the –
a. Human Resource Management Officer provide measurement on the earned
leave credits using Section 3.1 of DBM-BC No. 2016-2 dated March 29, 2016,

and (ii) disclose information related to employee leave benefits; and

credits for consistency of reports.

examination of the books of accounts of businesses, prioritizing those that consistently
fail to submit ITRs.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the
disclosure requirements of IPSAS 3.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the – 

MPDO, through the Acting Municipal Engineer to (i) perform detailed planning of the PPAs under the 20% Development Fund to ensure the feasibility of the projects; and (ii) monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time; and

Bids and Awards Committee and the Implementing Offices to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave way for their timely implementation and completion.

MDRRMO to ensure continuous optimal utilization of the 70% MF through the full implementation of the projects and activities in accordance with Republic Act No. 10121 to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks; 

MSWDO and other implementing offices to ensure proper inventory management of welfare goods to avoid wastage or loss; and

Average Method of costing the inventory;

be reconciled with Stock Cards (SCs) of the GSO; and
• record the distributions/issuances of supplies based on the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and other supporting documents
from end-users that would warrant the accuracy of the entry made.

We recommended and Management agreed to –
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure the proper use of correct asset
accounts in classifying various PPE acquisitions and adjust identified errors
herein; (ii) refrain from treating as expense account significant Repairs and
Materials costs and capitalize the expenditures identified herein; (iii) prepare
depreciation for all Infrastructure Assets and make an adjusting entry to
reflect its correct carrying amount; and (iv) expedite the completion of

year; and (iii) consider conducting one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
e) Instruct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to facilitate disposal of
unserviceable assets pursuant to Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the guidelines
under COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the granting of cash advance and its liquidation
and COA Circular No. 2015-009 through:
a) Instructing the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Budget Officer, and the

b) Designating additional disbursing officers to prevent granting of multiple cash
advances to one disbursing officer only;
c) Instructing the Municipal Accountant to (i) refrain from processing single

of cash advances in the proper accounts as prescribed under Annex B of COA
Circular No. 2015-009 prescribing the Chart of Accounts for Local
Government Units; and

them within the prescribed timeline.

for fund availability and consider the update and adjustment of daily minimum wage

private sector, subject to available funding requirements.

COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015; and
b) Ensure that all cash advances for Confidential Fund covered by the current
Joint Circular shall be liquidated at year-end.

to:

outlay from the general fund to the special trust fund;

outlay for LGU’s disaster risk reduction and management programs;
c) In coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, revert the unutilized balance of
special trust fund aged more than five years amounting to ₱1,998,727.96 to the
unappropriated surplus of general fund;

on or before 15th day after the end of each month; and
e) Submit the lacking supporting documents for the transactions charged to the
5% LDRRMF.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant
in coordination with the Inventory Committee to –
a) Review the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment
(RPCPPE) for CY 2023 to ascertain that insurable government properties
physically identified, especially high-value and high-risk properties are
insured with the GSIS; and

April 30 of each year.

We recommended and Management agreed to –
a) Instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal
Budget Officer to (i) ensure that the development projects to be funded out of
the 20% Development Fund are well-planned and procurement-and-
implementation-ready; and (ii) ensure that the 20% Development Fund shall
be utilized only for the priority development projects, pursuant to Items 3.2.1
and 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020;
b) Direct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the contractor regarding the
seven uninstalled solar lights; and
c) Revisit the MOA with Metro Clark Waste Management Corporation and
consider the computation based on actual volume of residual wastes per
container, or other more feasible and economical terms on the part of the
government.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the –
a) Business Permit and Licensing Officer and Municipal Treasurer to (i) remind
the businesses/taxpayers on the submission of certified copies of their income
tax returns (ITR) on or before April 30 of each year as required by the
Municipality’s Revenue Code; and (ii) if possible, request from the Bureau of
Internal Revenue a list of all registered business establishments in Morong,
Bataan with their declared gross sales for CY 2022 copy furnish the Audit
Team; and
b) Municipal Treasurer or authorized personnel in the Municipal Treasurer’s
Office to (i) attend a formal training on the examination of books of accounts
and pertinent records of businesses; and (ii) conduct the examination pursuant
to Section 171 of the Local Government Code.

We recommended and Management through its GFPS agreed to –
a) Ensure that the PPAs are in alignment with the GAD objectives of truly
addressing gender issues and concerns;
b) Inform the concerned DILG Office in writing regarding the changes in the
DILG-endorsed GPB to ensure that the actual result conformed with
established Performance Indicator and Target; and
c) Ensure accurate reporting of GAD AR by incorporating changes made to the
GAD Budget from realignments and supplemental budget.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal
Engineer and other concerned officials to provide the Audit Team at the beginning of
the year with the list of all ongoing projects/programs/activities (PPA) and those that
are to be implemented during the year with the required detail. Submit also to the
Audit Team information and evidence that appropriate signboards and/or public
notices were already posted 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or
before the start of PPA.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Bids and
Awards Committee and General Services Office to submit all copies of perfected
contracts with all the required supporting documents within five days from perfection
and all documents pertaining to delivery and acceptance of procured items.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, in compliance with the revised
IRR of RA No. 9184, agreed to –
a) Discontinue the conduct of   procurement activities not included in the
Agency’s APP to avoid future audit disallowance;
b) Direct all personnel concerned to prepare and submit APP and PPMP to
the GPPB in accordance with Section 7 of IRR of RA No. 9184, copy
furnish the Audit Team;
c) Direct the BAC to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR)
within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester and submit it to
GPBB, copy furnish the Audit Team; and
d) Instruct the BAC Secretariat to comply with the posting requirements of
IRR of RA No. 9184 such as the posting of an Invitation to Bid, Notice of
Award, Notice to Proceed, and Contract on the agency’s website.

We recommended and Management agreed to –
a) Require the Screening Committee to exercise their function specifically in the
validation of the authenticity of the submitted documents of the applicants;
b) Discontinue assisting students who are not indigent and those able to study in
private schools in line with Section 447(5)(xi) of RA No. 7160;
c) Require submission of certified copies of documents to avoid acceptance of
falsified documents;
d) Maintain a database of all the beneficiaries and status thereof for easy
monitoring and to measure the effectiveness of the Municipality’s program; and
e) Consider revisiting provisions of Pambayang Kapasyahan Blg. 22 Serye ng
2021 to rectify items A and E, as warranted.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Engineer to –
a) Strictly monitor the compliance of the contractors with the Construction
Safety and Health Program in the implementation of government projects
specifically the actual presence of the safety officer and first aider throughout
the project duration;
b) Explain the non-inclusion of the safety officer and first aider in the manpower
schedule and daily logbook of attendance; and
c) Submit to the Audit Team the CHSP of the other nine projects for further
validation.

We commended the Municipality for the timely implementation of the projects
for the benefit of its constituents. Likewise, we recommended and the Municipal
Mayor agreed to i) direct the Municipal Budget Officer to be informed from time to
time about the latest Local Budget Circular related to the transfer of funds received
by the Municipality to ensure its proper implementation specifically the posting and
reporting requirements; and ii) instruct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with
the contractor regarding the two uninstalled solar lights.

We also recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to (i) ensure the correctness of entries made in the books of accounts; (ii)
remit the ending balance of the “Due to BIR” account based on reconciliation made;
and (iii) see to it that all remittances are made within the deadlines set by the BIR.

We also recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to (i) ensure the correctness of entries made in the books of accounts; (ii)
remit the ending balance of the “Due to GSIS” account based on reconciliation made; and

Accountant (i) remit the ending balance of the “Due to Pag-IBIG” account based on
reconciliation made; and (ii) see to it that all remittances are made within the
deadlines set by the Pag-IBIG.

We also recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to (i) ensure the correctness of entries made in the books of accounts; (ii)
remit the ending balance of the “Due to PhilHealth” account based on reconciliation
made; and (iii) see to it that all remittances are made within the deadlines set by the
PHIC.

accounting, budgeting, and auditing rules and regulations to prevent audit
suspension, disallowance and charges.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to effect the immediate recording of valid reconciling
items in the books of accounts to ensure the correctness of the account
balances appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements at any given
time;
b. Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to
cause the timely submission of the disbursement vouchers and its
supporting documents of the checks issued and released to the
payee/suppliers; and
c. henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011
dated October 2, 1996 on the preparation of the bank reconciliation
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of the
entire Municipality’s PPE and correspondingly prepare and submit to the
Audit Team the required inventory report thereon;
b. direct the GSO and the Property Officer to ensure that Property Cards and
PARs are completely maintained and updated to facilitate the physical count
of all the Municipality’s properties. Also, determine the officials/employees
to whom the equipment was issued and issue the corresponding PAR for
each property to pinpoint accountability and responsibility. Lastly, assure
that PARs are renewed at least once in every three years or every time there
is a change in custodian of the property;
c. direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Administrator, as GSO-
Designate, to conduct the required reconciliation of their records with the
inventory report and adjust their records, if necessary, to present the true
balance of PPE accounts in the financial Statements; and
d. henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of the above-mentioned
regulations to come up with a reliable PPE balance.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to continue exerting efforts to trace back and substantiate the old
balances of the PPE accounts to provide the correct depreciation for each type
of property of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a. direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for
the proper recording of RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year
based on the RPT Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card;
b. direct the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accounting Office the
certified list of delinquent taxpayers to recognize the delinquent taxes from
CY 2019 backwards in the Municipality’s books; and
c. strictly comply with the above regulations to ensure fair presentation of the
Municipality’s RPT/SET Receivables in the financial statements.

We strongly reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a. coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from
and Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments could be made on
the affected accounts for fair presentation in the Municipality’s financial
statements; and
b. henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation of the account balances of Due
from LGUs and Due to LGUs in the Municipality’s books against their
reciprocal accounts reflected in the books of the Province to avoid the
accumulation of discrepancies between the two records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

disposal of all the accounted unserviceable properties following the guidelines
set forth under COA Circular No. 92-386;

determine the details of 320 unaccounted unserviceable properties; and
c. Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the
derecognition of all unserviceable properties in the Municipality’s books once
disposed of and record accordingly the income that could be derived
therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat to strictly
adhere to the guidelines in the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan for
proper monitoring and achievement of the strategic plan of the entire
procurement activities for goods and services and infrastructure of the
Municipality to be undertaken within the calendar year.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations and Management agreed to
instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) strictly monitor and conduct regular and
periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables and
payable accounts; (b) conduct reconciliation of the balances to source agencies in
case of payable accounts and; (c) request for write-off of uncollectible receivables
and payables from the books of accounts to the Commission on Audit supported by
documents required by the Circular.

We recommended and Management agreed to –
a. ensure the (i) preparation of RPCI in the required format as certified
correct by the Inventory Committee, attested by the Audit Team’s
representative and approved by the Head of the Agency and (ii)
submission of RPCI to the Audit Team for audit and validation thereof;
b. direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) assign a staff who will be
responsible in the preparation and maintenance of SLCs and compute the
cost of each item using moving average method (except for drugs and
medicines with expiration period); and (ii) use perpetual inventory
method instead of direct expense in order to properly account all the
unused supplies and/or materials as of a given date;
c. require the Inventory Custodian/AO to update SCs daily and prepare
SSMI to be submitted every week or at least once a month to the
Municipal Accountant together with RIS or equivalent document so that
issuances will be computed and recorded by the latter;
d. instruct the GSO to guarantee that all food, supplies, drugs and
medicines whether purchased by the Municipality or donated by other
agencies are recorded by the Municipal Accountant by furnishing
delivery receipt and/or Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR); and
e. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to apply for the fidelity bond of
Inventory Custodians who are regular employees of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to separate the personnel
composition of the Inventory and the Disposal and Appraisal Committee as well as
their assigned duties and responsibilities to ensure the functionality of the responsible
committees in compliance with the following:

a) Inventory Committee to –
• conduct complete annual physical count of PPE;
• reconcile the actual count and the existing inventory records, if there
is any, and submit complete and duly signed RPCPPE to the Office of
the Auditor not later than the prescribed deadline; and
• identify the reasons for the differences noted before making any
adjustment; and
b) Disposal and Appraisal Committee to –
• inspect unserviceable equipment and property to verify justification for
disposal;
• set the final appraised value of all disposable properties;
• recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) for approval, the
manner of disposal; and
• conduct public bidding for the sale of disposable property on an ‘as is,
where is basis’ and recommend corresponding award thereof.

We also reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed
to instruct the -
a. OIC-GSO to –
• cause the preparation and maintenance of complete and updated
Property Cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger
balances and the inventory report; and
• take the necessary action to identify all unserviceable government
properties and prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) so that proper disposal could be
undertaken; and
b. Municipal Accountant make necessary adjusting entry to record the
derecognition of properties after disposal for proper presentation of the
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to –
a) consider the application of postpaid plans under a
corporate/enterprise account of Municipality of Morong to a
telecommunication service provider for ease in monitoring and control of
mobile expenses;
b) thru the Sanggunian Bayan, include in their resolution that the mobile
allowance granted to officials and department heads of the Municipality shall
be subjected to budgeting, accounting and auditing rules and regulations; and
c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the required
supporting documents for mobile allowance are attached to the DV to
support the validity of the claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the
Municipality’s PPE and prepare and submit the required report thereon, in
proper form and within the prescribed period. Any discrepancy between the
inventory report and the accounting and property records should be
investigated and make the necessary adjustments where the error was
committed;
b. Municipal Accountant and the GSO to ensure that PPE Ledger Cards and
Property Cards were being maintained, respectively. Both records should
always be updated and reconciled;
c. Officials concerned to submit the required additional documents to support
the request to write off the properties razed/damaged by the fire; and
d. Municipal Accountant to continue exerting efforts to trace back and
substantiate the old balances of the PPE accounts in order to provide the
correct depreciation for each type of properties of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to correct the
records and balances of the Road Network Systems and its related
Accumulated Depreciation and Depreciation Expense accounts;
b. GSO to maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map, LRNPC and RLRN in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008; and
c. Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the
total road network system of the Municipality as required in COA Circular
No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to make certain that transmittal to the Accounting Office
of transaction documents are timely made; and
b. Municipal Accountant to effect the immediate recording of transactions in
the books of accounts to ensure the correctness of the account balances
appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements at any given time.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. submit immediately the above-mentioned reports/accounts and their
supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor to enable the Audit Team
to conduct the review and verification of the same. Otherwise, the
corresponding Notice of Suspension and Notice of Charge shall be issued
for its non-submission; and
b. ensure that all disbursement and collection reports and their supporting
documents are submitted within the prescribed period as required under the
aforesaid regulations.

We strongly reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the
reconciliation of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate
adjustments could be made on the affected accounts for fair presentation in the
Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
submit to the Audit Team the documents supporting the Prior Period
Adjustments account for verification and ensure that submission of all vouchers
are supported with valid documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. strictly comply with the rules on the setting-up of the RPT and SET
Receivables to ensure the fair presentation of the Municipality’s financial
statements; and
b. draw the necessary adjusting entries to correct the affected account
balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the implementing division
and/or the personnel assigned to fast track the implementation and
accomplishment of the projects so that the benefits would be delivered to the
intended beneficiaries at the soonest possible time. Otherwise, the unutilized
funds must be returned to the NGAs in order that it may be made available for
other priority programs or projects of said government agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
the designated Municipal GSO to:
a. submit the required documents on the above-mentioned lapses for
evaluation of this Audit Team; and
b. process and pay only claims with complete documentation pursuant to the
above regulation and other existing government accounting and auditing
rules to avoid audit suspension and/or audit disallowances.

observations.recommendation:
We commended Management for the 

(a) creation of monitoring and evaluation team that will determine if policies, structures, systems and mechanisms were put in place to institutionalize the pursuit of gender equality and women’s empowerment;  

(b) formulation of policies in advancing women’s status such as the GAD Code and 

(c) through the GFPS, establishment of the GAD database as basis for planning, budgeting and policy formulation in accordance with the mandates of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women.

We also recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE assign a different leader to man the GFPS or the Monitoring and Evaluation Team in order to make valuable assessments of the implemented programs to ensure that projects embodied in the GAD Plan and Budget do address specific gender issues.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has attained compliance with GAD mechanisms and processes.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented

LGU’s GAD Code is yet to be formulated and the GAD database which was not yet fully in place.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:  Officials concerned to justify the inclusion of the P2,685,502.88 worth of properties in their request for write-off of the properties razed/damaged by fire considering these properties were unsubstantiated and no details were available on their records to support such balance;  Inventory Committee to conduct a physical inventory of all the Municipality’s PPE and prepare and submit the required report thereon. Any discrepancies between the inventory report and the accounting and property records should be investigated and make the necessary adjustments where the error was committed; and  Municipal Accountant to continue exerting efforts to trace back and substantiate the old balances of the PPE accounts in order to provide the correct depreciation for each type of properties of the Municipality; d. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment to correct the discrepancies made on the Accumulated Depreciation - Road Network System and Depreciation Expense account; and f. Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the total road network system as required in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: Management reasoned the lack of personnel to handle the records and substantiate the movement of PPE balances and validate the existences of PPE items. Inventory Committee has started conducting the required inventory taking. Municipal Accountant is continuously working out on the reconciliation of Physical Inventory available on file with PPE recorded in the books.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management. For the partial implementa- tion of the audit recommendation the Management is continuously working to locate the records for the partial implementa- tion of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a regular reconciliation of their respective records and immediately effect the necessary adjustments to avoid the accumulation of the discrepancy, as well as to ensure the correctness of the account balances appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management, thru the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, agreed to prepare and submit regularly to the Audit Team the required reconciliation report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:  Ensure that the said reports and their supporting documents are submitted to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period to facilitate the review and verification of the same and the timely preparation of the required audit reports;  Determine the cause/s of the delay in the submission of the accounts of the Municipality to the Auditor and find solutions thereto;  Submit to the Audit Team the corresponding DVs for CY 2017 and Liquidation Reports for post-audit. Otherwise, any unjustified failure on the part of the official concerned to submit the required reports shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of his salaries until he shall have complied therewith without prejudice to disciplinary action that may be instituted against him, pursuant to Section 122 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant assured compliance with the audit recommendations. The Audit Team acknowledged the commitment of the Municipal Accountant to comply and will continuously monitor Management’s compliance with the submission of accounts within the period prescribed by regulations.
observations.reason: Most of the newly bidded infrastructure projects were submitted to the Audit Team. However, still not compliant with the required reports.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to closely coordinate with each other to prepare and submit the certified list of taxpayers and the tax collectible from each at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Management assured compliance with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to do the task. Management has not yet created dedicated personnel and has considered the hiring or assigning personnel who will exclusively work on the task while the computerized RPT system has not yet materialize to resolve the concern of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office in compliance with the requirements of the regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to continue to exert diligent efforts in locating the records pertaining to the lease agreement in order to establish the validity and legality of the transaction as well as to enforce the payment and file appropriate charges where necessary.
observations.managementAction: Management, thru the Municipal Accountant, committed to comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Management is continuously exerting efforts to locate the whereabouts of the lessee contractor. Management has not obtained a copy of the lease agreement, since the Municipality’s claim happened during the time of the previous administration and enforcement of the charge happened during prior period.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records in coordination with the Provincial Accountant in order to remit the total amount due once the correct balances have been established.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has coordinated with the Provincial Accountant. The Municipality has already remitted portions and is in the process of reconciling the said accounts with the Provincial Accounting Office.

observations.reason: No reason was provided for the non-implementation. It was reiterated in audit observation no. 9, page no. 47.

observations.managementAction: Management has provided documents for newly bidded infrastructure projects to the Audit Team. Municipal Engineer and the GSO are in the process of reconciling and preparing the detailed descriptions and cost segregation.
observations.reason: No reason was provided by Management for the non-implementation of some items. Some infrastructure projects were submitted for audit but still not compliant with the required reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to prepare and submit all the information and reports required under the above-cited circular to promote transparency and to facilitate the validation of the PPAs undertaken by the Municipality and to submit the same to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Management took steps and submitted documents for some newly bidded projects. However, still not compliant with all the required reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal officials concerned to strictly adhere to the guidelines provided under the Joint Memorandum Circulars to ensure efficient output, as well as come up with projects with developmental impact that would improve the socio-economic condition of its constituents; and b. Municipal Budget Officer to replenish the 20% DF from the General Fund Proper in the amount expended for expenses which do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. Otherwise, these transactions shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management took note of the audit recommendations and committed to pass an SB Resolution that would replenish the 20% Development Fund of the amount disbursed on programs/activities which did not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. Management also indicated adjustments already made such as realignment of MOOE and replenishment through SB Resolution No. 2, s. 2017 for part of the amount.
observations.reason: No reason was provided by Management for the partial implementation. Some amounts have been replenished; other amounts remain subject to adjustment.

observations.reason: There is a need for an additional budget for two pieces of water source pipes to get the water source component into the operational stage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GFPS to fully implement the GAD Plan and Budget so that the intent of GAD on promoting gender-responsive governance, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, and promoting women’s empowerment could be fully achieved; and to strictly monitor the implementation of the planned GAD program, projects and activities to ensure that the GAD objectives are fully attained and the actual expenditures will not exceed the approved budget per PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Management assured compliance and indicated that the GAD Focal Person continuously monitors the Municipality’s GAD related PPAs and will implement remaining activities.
observations.reason: No reason was provided by Management for partial implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. instruct the LDRRMO to prepare and furnish the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) and the DILG-MLGOO a copy of a comprehensive annual LDRRMFIP in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 and ensure that the prior years’ unexpended balances are incorporated on the said plan; and b. abide with DILG MC No. 2012-73 in utilizing the 70% Mitigation Fund and maximize the utilization thereof to fully achieve the benefits of the programs, projects and activities funded therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to observe due diligence in the preparation/formulation of their LDRRMFIP, and promised to strictly observe the required submission of reports to the OCD and DILG. LDRRMO agreed concerned Offices have to meet and coordinate regularly on a monthly basis to prepare the required reports and to reconcile balances as reflected in their reports.
observations.reason: The Municipality submitted a LDRRM Investment Plan but not compliant with COA Circular No. 2012-002; some utilization was made from prior years balances without proper identification of projects in the plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. compel the concerned Municipal officials to strictly observe the guidelines in the utilization of the LDRRMF provided under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 to ensure that the LDRRMF is utilized solely for approved disaster risk reduction and management (DRRM) related programs, projects and activities (PPAs); and b. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to replenish the LDRRMF from the General Fund in the amount expended for non-related disaster risk reduction management activities. Otherwise, these transactions shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management replenished the LDRRMF from the General Fund in the amount of P62,051.57 and implemented controls to ensure future compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) take advantage of the newly installed Property Assessment and Tax Administration System (PATAS) and find ways of the possibility to link the system with the Municipal Treasurer Office; (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the Certified List of Taxpayers as basis to take up RPT/SET Receivables in the books; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SL) that will monitor tax collections and delinquencies in compliance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Use of Property Assessment and Tax Administration System will be implemented in 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) create an inventory committee to conduct annual physical inventory of PPE and submit the RPCPPE to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; and (b)  require the (i) Municipal Accountant to record the necessary adjustments/corrections so as to come up with the correct figures and maintain updated PPELC/RPLC;  and (ii) Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards and reconcile the data therein with the inventory reports and the general ledger to validate the reported balances.
observations.managementAction: Inventory team will be created.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) in coordination with Municipal Development Council, optimize the utilization of the 20% development fund on PPAs outlined under DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 to achieve the desired benefits in socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes; (b) require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts under General Fund for development projects for proper accountability and control of the use the fund and to observe the proper classification of accounts; and (c) require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds for projects covered under 20% development fund in accordance with Section 3 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: MDC is monitoring utilization of projects under 20% development fund; Accounting Office will maintain special book of accounts for development projects under 20% development fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that Management (a) create monitoring and evaluation team that will determine if policies, structures, systems and mechanisms were put in place to institutionalize the pursuit of gender equality and women’s empowerment; (b) ensure that projects embodied in the GAD Plan and Budget do address specific gender issues; (c) through the SB to formulate policies in advancing women’s status such as the GAD Code and (d) through the GFPS, established the GAD database as basis for planning, budgeting and policy formulation in accordance with the mandates of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to (a) hire additional personnel at the MTO department in lieu of the two personnel assigned in the collection and encoding of RPT/SET Receivable that will retire this year; (b) take advantage of the installed Property Assessment and Tax Administration System (PATAS) and maximize its use at the Municipal Treasurer Office; and (c) comply with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual on the setting up of Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivable. The Treasurer upon completion of the Certified List of Taxpayers should be submitted to the Accountant. Based on this, the Accountant should take up in the books, the RPT/SET Receivable with the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SL) that will monitor tax collections and delinquencies.

We also recommended and the Management committed to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer maintain updated PPELC/RPLC and property cards, respectively, and to reconcile the data and figures therein with the inventory reports and the general ledger in order to validate the reported balances.

We recommended that Members of the Municipal Local School Board (a) prepare a SEF budget in compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; (b) request for inputs from the Deped official/Schools Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund; and (c) maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the implementation of the PPAs, and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprograming, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: SEF budget hearing should be conducted to ensure that guidelines are met.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) refrain from using DRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly related with disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations; (b) adhere to the accounting and reporting guidelines of the LDRRMF to properly account for the expenses related thereto; and (c) direct the OIC-MDRRMO to (i) prepare a certified monthly Report on the Utilization of DRRMF and submit to  COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and (ii) monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF based on the programs, projects and activities listed in the LDRRMIP as incorporated in the annual investment plan to ensure the full implementation of the pre-disaster preparedness programs.
observations.managementAction: Related projects to disaster preparedness will be analyzed according to guidelines; Regular reporting by DRRMO will be required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that Management thru the (a) MSWD, establish clear-cut policy on monitoring and implementation of SEA Kaunlaran project and install proper control in recording and deposit of collections to avoid exposing the funds to possible loss, misuse and misappropriation; and (b) BUB focal person submit assessment/evaluation report for every BUB project in accordance with Section 4.7.3 of DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Memorandum Circular No. 6 to evaluate if the objectives in the identified projects were met.
observations.managementAction: SEA K proceeds on loan are deposited to Morong Multi Purpose Coop then use the funds for relending to either same group in good credit standing or in a new identify group.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that Management comply with COA Circular No. 2014-003 pertaining to Information and Publicity of Program/Projects/Activities of Government Agencies so that policy on full disclosure is adopted.
observations.managementAction: This is properly noted and recommendations are being followed. Requirements for contractors are implemented. Invitations to bid are posted in PhilGEPS and conspicuous places.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended and Management agreed that the Marhalls strictly monitor cases of tricycles overloading, parking on roads, kolorums and other violations to prevent undesirable outcomes as a result of unregulated operations thereof. We further recommended that the SB revisit/review the enacted ordinances to include other provisions relevant to operations of three-wheeled vehicles for hire.
observations.managementAction: The municipal DRRMO who is in charge of traffice management regularly meet with marshalls to monitor strict compliance with ordinances, specially those involving three-wheeled motor vehicles (tricycles). Check tricycles operating whether they have updated permits and impose penalties for violators.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) through the Solid Waste Management Board, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; (b) through the Sangguniang Bayan (SB), adopt an ordinance pertaining to policies and possible measures that will ensure full compliance by the barangays on the provisions of RA No. 9003, especially on the operation of a Sanitary Landfill; (c) take appropriate action to ensure that the existing MRF of the three component barangays be fully functional; (d) provide necessary support in enjoining the remaining barangays to establish their own MRF; and (e) put signage that the open dumpsite is already closed and have the area secured from unauthorized persons.
observations.managementAction: Continuous IEC are being done by the members of the ESWM Vboard with other stakeholders; 4 out of 5 barangay have MRFs; Ordinances are in place and barangays are expected to be on top of its implementation; Construction of landfill is not possible for now, thus the service of DENR licensed hauler, Metro Clark was commissioned; The open dumpsite was closed in accordance with DENR-EMB guidelines.
observations.reason: IEC strategies not enough to encourage residents participation; Penalties for violators of ordinance partially implemented.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended that Management (a) take advantage of the newly installed
Property Assessment and Tax Administration System (PATAS) and find ways of the
possibility to link the system with the Municipal Treasurer Office; (b) require the
Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the Certified List of
Taxpayers as basis to take up RPT/SET Receivables in the books; and (c) require
the Municipal Accountant to maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SL)
that will monitor tax collections and delinquencies in compliance with Section 20 of
the NGAS Manual.

2.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) create an inventory
committee to conduct annual physical inventory of PPE and submit the RPCPPE to
the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; and (b) require the (i)
Municipal Accountant to record the necessary adjustments/corrections so as to
come up with the correct figures and maintain updated PPELC/RPLC; and (ii)
Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards and reconcile the data therein with
the inventory reports and the general ledger to validate the reported balances.

3.6 We recommended that Members of the Municipal Local School Board (a)
prepare a SEF budget in compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements and
refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has
the direct mandate to execute; (b) request for inputs from the Deped official/Schools
Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in
accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws,
rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund; and (c)
maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the implementation
of the PPAs, and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprograming, if
necessary.

4.5 We recommended that Management (a) refrain from using DRRMF for
programs/projects/activities which are not directly related with disaster
preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations; (b) adhere to the
accounting and reporting guidelines of the LDRRMF to properly account for the
expenses related thereto; and (c) direct the OIC-MDRRMO to (i) prepare a certified
monthly Report on the Utilization of DRRMF and submit to COA on or before the
15th day after the end of each month; and (ii) monitor and evaluate the utilization of
LDRRMF based on the programs, projects and activities listed in the LDRRMIP as
incorporated in the annual investment plan to ensure the full implementation of the
pre-disaster preparedness programs.

5.17 We recommended and Management agreed that Management thru the (a)
MSWD, establish clear-cut policy on monitoring and implementation of SEA
Kaunlaran project and install proper control in recording and deposit of collections
to avoid exposing the funds to possible loss, misuse and misappropriation; and (b)
BUB focal person submit assessment/evaluation report for every BUB project in
accordance with Section 4.7.3 of DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Memorandum Circular
No. 6 to evaluate if the objectives in the identified projects were met.

6.6 We also recommended that Management comply with COA Circular No.
2014-003 pertaining to Information and Publicity of Program/Projects/Activities of
Government Agencies so that policy on full disclosure is adopted.

7.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) in coordination with
Municipal Development Council, optimize the utilization of the 20% development
fund on PPAs outlined under DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1
to achieve the desired benefits in socio-economic development and environmental
management outcomes; (b) require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special
accounts under General Fund for development projects for proper accountability
and control of the use the fund and to observe the proper classification of accounts;
and (c) require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds for projects
covered under 20% development fund in accordance with Section 3 of DILG-DBM
JMC No. 2011-1.

8.22 We also recommended and Management agreed that the Marhalls strictly
monitor cases of tricycles overloading, parking on roads, kolorums and other
violations to prevent undesirable outcomes as a result of unregulated operations
thereof. We further recommended that the SB revisit/review the enacted ordinances
to include other provisions relevant to operations of three-wheeled vehicles for hire.

9.11 We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
(a) through the Solid Waste Management Board, encourage active participation of
barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the
government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of
reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; (b) through the
Sangguniang Bayan (SB), adopt an ordinance pertaining to policies and possible
measures that will ensure full compliance by the barangays on the provisions of RA
No. 9003, especially on the operation of a Sanitary Landfill; (c) take appropriate
action to ensure that the existing MRF of the three component barangays be fully
functional; (d) provide necessary support in enjoining the remaining barangays to
establish their own MRF; and (e) put signage that the open dumpsite is already
closed and have the area secured from unauthorized persons.

10.11 We recommended and Management agreed that Management (a) create
monitoring and evaluation team that will determine if policies, structures, systems
and mechanisms were put in place to institutionalize the pursuit of gender equality
and women’s empowerment; (b) ensure that projects embodied in the GAD Plan
and Budget do address specific gender issues; (c) through the SB to formulate
policies in advancing women’s status such as the GAD Code and (d) through the
GFPS, established the GAD database as basis for planning, budgeting and policy
formulation in accordance with the mandates of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna
Carta of Women.

13.3 We recommended that Management remain compliant with laws,
accounting, budgeting and auditing rules and regulations and act swiftly on the
noted deviations in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require both the Accountant and the Treasurer to conduct a regular or monthly reconciliation of their respective cash in bank account records, identify all reconciling items and make the necessary corrections/adjustments thereto as well as to those other affected accounts to ensure the correctness of the account balances being reported in the financial statements.

We strongly reiterated that Honorable Municipal Mayor addresses the lack of manpower in the Treasury Office and requires the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the certified list of taxpayers and the tax collectible from each at the beginning of each year, so that the corresponding receivable accounts can be appropriately set up at the beginning of the year.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exhausts all the necessary measures to determine the details of the delinquent property taxes totaling P 34,379,691.70 for CYs 2012 – 2016 and make a complete report to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording of the same in the books.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Mayor agreed that they will exhaust all necessary efforts to locate the records regarding the lease. Coordinate with the previous officials of the Municipality who may have knowledge on the transaction in locating the documents, so that collection of the unpaid rentals could be enforced, including the filing of charges against the firm.
We reiterated also that there should always be a proper turn-over of accountabilities, not only for cash and property, but also of all vital records, whenever there are movements/changes of officials and employees in the Agency, to ensure that all important documents are properly filed and intact and in order to establish proper accountabilities thereof.

We strongly reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the concerned officials to prepare and submit all the information and reports required under the above-cited Circular to promote transparency and accountability regarding the PPAs undertaken by the Municipality and facilitate the validation of the same by the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor, as the Chairperson in the LPRAT, requires the designated BUB Focal Person to immediately submit the said required documents to facilitate the audit of the same by the Audit Team. Otherwise, failure to do so, a Notice of Suspension will be issued.

We recommended that the Municipal Bids and Awards Committee submit justification and other documents necessary to prove the propriety of resorting to Direct Contracting in the procurement of said equipment.
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately effect the adjusting entry for the erroneous recording of the procurement of equipment in order to correct the balances of the affected accounts and avoid possible loss or misuse of the subject equipment.

We recommended the settlement of the disallowed transactions by the municipal officials and employees concerned. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from paying additional allowances or bonuses when the 45% Personnel Services limitation is exceeded.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the:
Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that  only investment or capital expenditures are charged to the 20%  Development Fund; and
Municipal Accountant to observe the proper classification of transactions in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard.

We recommended that the (a)
MSWDO and other            
implementing departments   
such as Offices of the     
Municipal Accountant,      
Treasurer and Budget,      
review, consolidate and    
finalize the policy        
guidelines on the grant of

forms of financial         
assistance to be reviewed  
by the Municipal Mayor for 
approval of the Sanggunian 
Bayan; and (b) Municipal   
Accountant ensure that     
expenses are charged to the
proper accounts.
